conversation.text_en: Hi, I'm your therapist. Everything we discuss here is confidential, so please feel comfortable.
Thanks, I appreciate that.
Sorry to hear that. Can you tell me a bit about your week? What's been going on?
Same old same old. Work's been keepin' me busy, trying to meet deadlines.
How's work as an electrician treating you lately?
It's just been tough, ya know? Trying to get everything done on time and people are always calling in last minute requests.
I understand. Do you have any downtime or relaxation activities that help you unwind?
Honestly, no. I mean, if I'm not working, I'm usually just trying to catch up on sleep.
Sleep has been a challenge lately, huh? How many hours are you getting each night?
Like three, four tops. And even then it's not great, I wake up feeling like I've got cottonmouth.
That sounds rough. Have you noticed any changes in your mood or energy levels recently?
Yeah, I'm just kinda... done. You know? Like, all the motivation and enthusiasm I used to have is gone.
I hear that. What are some things you used to enjoy doing that you haven't had the energy for lately?
Um, I don't know... I mean, I used to love fishing, but it's just too exhausting now.
It sounds like it's been a real struggle. Can you tell me more about what's going on at work that's causing so much stress?
Just the constant pressure to meet deadlines and deal with difficult clients all the time.
That can be overwhelming. Have you talked to your supervisor or HR about how you're feeling?
Nah, I don't wanna seem weak or like I'm whining. Just need to buck up and get it done, ya know?
I understand that feeling. Sometimes it's hard to ask for help when we feel like we should be able to handle things on our own.
Yeah, exactly. So what can we do about this? How are you gonna fix me?
Well, let's start by exploring some coping strategies and stress management techniques that might help you feel more grounded.
Sounds good. I guess it couldn't hurt to try.
That's a great attitude! What's been the most challenging part of your daily routine lately?
Honestly? Just getting out of bed in the morning. It takes me like an hour and a half just to get moving.
I hear that. Can you tell me what's going on during those early morning hours that makes it so hard for you?
I don't know... maybe I'm just not liking the person I've become, ya know? Feeling stuck and unfulfilled.
That can be a really tough place to be in. Would you like to explore what's been going on during those early morning hours a bit more?
Yeah, that'd be helpful. Maybe I'm just not liking the person I've become because I'm not living the life I want to live.
That makes sense. What does living the life you want to live look like for you?
Um... well, I guess it would mean having more energy and motivation, enjoying my hobbies again, and being able to tackle challenges without feeling overwhelmed.
Those are all great goals! Let's work on some strategies to help you get there. How about we start with some small changes you can make in your daily routine?
Sounds good. Like what?
Well, maybe taking a short walk during your lunch break or setting aside 10 minutes each morning for yourself before getting started.
Yeah, that could work. I'll try it.
That's great! What do you think would be the biggest challenge in making these changes stick?
Hmm... probably just getting out of bed on time, ya know? It's like my alarm clock is telling me to wake up and start my day already!
I hear that. Sometimes it can be tough to get started. Let's make a plan to help you with that too.
Sounds good. Like what?
Well, maybe setting your alarm clock 15 minutes earlier each day or doing something relaxing before getting out of bed, like deep breathing exercises.
Yeah, that could work. I'll try it.
That's great! What do you think is the most important thing for us to focus on in our sessions together?
Honestly? Just getting some energy back and enjoying my life again.
That's a great goal. Let's work towards that. Is there anything else you'd like to talk about or any questions you have before we wrap up today?
Yeah, just one thing – how do I know if this is even working? How will I know when I'm getting better?
That's a great question! We can definitely track your progress and set some specific goals for ourselves. Let me show you some tools we can use to monitor that.
Sounds good.
Great! Well, that's all the time we have for today. Remember to take care of yourself this week and practice those relaxation techniques we talked about.
Yeah, will do. Thanks for listening.
